MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-14-2021 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., AND RASIER, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT; ARGUMENT IS HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED WITH ONE MODIFICATION: 4 (FRAUD). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PLEAD FRAUD WITH THE REQUIRED SPECIFICITY - "HOW, WHEN, WHERE, TO WHOM AND BY WHAT MEANS." (LAZAR V. SUP. CT. (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 631, 645.) THIS DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. 5-6, 8 (ASSAULT, BATTERY, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS). THIS DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. 10 (RALPH ACT). THIS DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. MR. ALARCON IS TO PREPARE A REVISED PROPOSED ORDER WITH THE MODIFICATION THAT REPLACES THE TEXT "DOE ONE" WITH THE NAME OF THE INDIVIDUAL. (SEE MINI-MINUTES THIS DATE FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. And Rasier, Llcs Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Linda I. Yen In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Walt Cubberly, Esq. of Williams Hart Boundas Easterby LLP, 713.230.2200, for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Gary Wolensky, Esq. of Buchalter, 949.760.1121, for the Defendants;
Atty. Paul Alarcon, Esq. of Buchalter, 949.760.1121, for the Defendants.

DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., AND RASIER, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's motion to strike is denied in part and granted in part. Plaintiff's "allegations for the purpose of prejudicing Uber" are colorful, but within the realm of acceptable pleading, particularly for background. Because the basis for vicarious liability is adequately pled on the basis of the common carrier allegations, that part of the motion is denied. Because the basis for punitive damages is pled with insufficient particularity, that part of the motion is granted with leave to amend. Presumably plaintiff will be amending her complaint to plead fraud with greater particularity, and fraud plays a prominent role in punitive damages law. (See, e.g., CACI 3946.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Walt Cubberly, Esq. of Williams Hart Boundas Easterby LLP, 713.230.2200, for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Gary Wolensky, Esq. of Buchalter, 949.760.1121, for the Defendants;
Atty. Paul Alarcon, Esq. of Buchalter, 949.760.1121, for the Defendants.

DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., AND RASIER, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT;

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted with a modification that replaces the text "Doe One" with the name of the individual:

4 (fraud). Plaintiff fails to plead fraud with the required specificity - "how, when, where, to whom and by what means." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) This demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

5-6, 8 (assault, battery, intentional infliction of emotional distress). Defendants' demurrer ignores the complaint's allegations that "Uber is a common carrier" and thus liable for assaults "regardless of whether such acts were committed within the course and scope of employment for Uber." (Cmpl., 19:8-17.) Defendants argue that "Uber cannot be held vicariously liable for Doe One's acts, even if he were an employee or agent (which he is not), since he acted on personal motivations unrelated to his duties as an ultrasound technician." (Dem., 8:23-25.) While Defendants' reply shows that some courts have sustained demurrers as to the scope of employment, the cases Defendants rely on are in the teacher/student and priest/parishioner contexts. Defendants do not show that the factual issues in this context can be resolved at the pleading stage. The complaint's allegations are sufficiently pled for factual development. This demurrer is overruled.

10 (Ralph Act). The first amended complaint does not adequately plead the elements of a Ralph Act claim as to the demurring defendants, and Plaintiff fails to show on opposition that she can amend to do so. (Cmpl., 20:9-21:5; CACI 3063.) This demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

Mr. Alarcon is to prepare a revised proposed order with the modification that replaces the text "Doe One" with the name of the individual.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)
